Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the National Defence Act and other Acts

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled âAn Act to amend the National Defence Act and other Actsâ.

This enactment amends provisions of the National Defence Act that relate to the military justice system in response to the Report of the Third Independent Review Authority to the Minister of National Defence and the Report of the Independent External Comprehensive Review of the Department of National Defence and the Canadian Armed Forces.
In response to those reports, the enactment amends that Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: modify the process for appointing the Canadian Forces Provost Marshal, the Director of Military Prosecutions and the Director of Defence Counsel Services with a view to enhancing their independence;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: affirm the Judge Advocate Generalâs respect for the independence of authorities in the military justice system in the exercise of the Judge Advocate Generalâs superintendence of the administration of military justice;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: remove the court martialâs jurisdiction to try a person in relation to an offence under the Criminal Code that is alleged to have been committed in Canada and that is of a sexual nature or committed for a sexual purpose;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: remove the Canadian Armed Forcesâ authority to investigate an offence under the Criminal Code that is alleged to have been committed in Canada and that is of a sexual nature or committed for a sexual purpose;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: expand the class of persons who are eligible to be appointed as a military judge;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: expand the class of persons who may make an interference complaint and provide that a member of the military police or person performing policing duties or functions under the Canadian Forces Provost Marshalâs supervision must make such a complaint in certain circumstances; and

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: change the title of the Canadian Forces Provost Marshal to the Provost Marshal General.

In addition, the enactment amends the National Defence Act to remove military judges from the summary hearing system and to provide that, in the context of a service offence, an individual acting on behalf of a victim may request that a victimâs liaison officer be appointed to assist them.

It further amends that Act to harmonize the sex offender information and publication ban provisions with the amendments made to the Criminal Code in An Act to amend the Criminal Code, the Sex Offender Information Registration Act and the International Transfer of Offenders Act.

Finally, it amends the Criminal Code to, among other things, provide superior courts of criminal jurisdiction with the jurisdiction to hear applications for an exemption in respect of orders to comply with the Sex Offender Information Registration Act made under the National Defence Act and applications to vary the duration of such orders.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Military Justice System Modernization Act.
Section 9.2 of the National Defence Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, in the exercise of the Judge Advocate Generalâs superintendence, the Judge Advocate General respects the independence of authorities in the military justice system, including the Provost Marshal General, the Director of Military Prosecutions and the Director of Defence Counsel Services.
Paragraph 12(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the rates and conditions of issue of pay of military judges;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 12(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Regulations made under paragraph (3)(a) may, if they so provide, have retroactive effect. However, they may not have effect

Section.Text: Subsections 18.3(1) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may appoint an officer who has been a member of the military police for at least 10 years to be the Provost Marshal General.
The Provost Marshal General holds a rank that is not less than brigadier-general.
The Provost Marshal General holds office during pleasure for a term not exceeding four years.

Section.Text: Paragraph 18.4(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the supervision of the performance of policing duties and functions;

Section.Text: Sections 18.5 and 18.6 of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Provost Marshal General is absent or unable to act or the office of Provost Marshal General is vacant, the Minister may authorize any officer who is a member of the military police to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions of the Provost Marshal General, but that officer may act as the Provost Marshal General for a period of more than 90 days only with the approval of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: The Provost Marshal General is responsible to the Minister in the performance of the responsibilities described in paragraphs 18.4(a) to (d).

The Minister may issue general instructions or guidelines in writing in respect of the responsibilities described in paragraphs 18.4(a) to (d). The Provost Marshal General shall ensure that they are available to the public.

Section.Text: The Provost Marshal General shall, within three months after the end of each fiscal year, submit to the Minister a report concerning the activities of the Provost Marshal General and the military police during the year.
Section 70 of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under any of the following provisions of the Criminal Code:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 153.1 (sexual exploitation of person with disability),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 160(3) (bestiality in presence of or by a child),

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: section 162.1 (publication, etc., of an intimate image without consent),

Subparagraph.Label: (xii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 170 (parent or guardian procuring sexual activity),

Subparagraph.Label: (xiv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 171.1 (making sexually explicit material available to child),

Subparagraph.Label: (xvi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 172.2 (agreement or arrangement â sexual offence against child),

Subparagraph.Label: (xix)
Subparagraph.Text: section 272 (sexual assault with a weapon, threats to a third party or causing bodily harm),

Subparagraph.Label: (xxi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 273.3 (removal of a child from Canada),

Subparagraph.Label: (xxiii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 279.02(2) (material benefit â trafficking of person under 18 years),

Subparagraph.Label: (xxiv)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 279.03(2) (withholding or destroying documents â trafficking of person under 18 years),

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under the Criminal Code â other than an offence referred to in paragraph (d) â that is of a sexual nature or that is committed for a sexual purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under the Criminal Code, as it read from time to time before the day on which this paragraph comes into force, if the conduct alleged would be an offence referred to in paragraph (d) or (e) if it occurred on or after that day;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: conspiring or attempting to commit an offence referred to in any of paragraphs (d) to (f) or being an accessory after the fact to such an offence; or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: counselling a person to commit an offence referred to in any of paragraphs (d) to (f) if the offence is not committed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 70:

Section.MarginalNote: No authority to investigate â certain offences
Section.Text: Despite any other provision of this Act and any other law, an officer or non-commissioned member does not have authority to investigate, for the purposes of the laying of a charge under paragraph 130(1)(a) or an information under the Criminal Code, in relation to an offence referred to in any of paragraphs 70(d) to (h) that was, or is alleged to have been, committed in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Nothing in section 70.1 prevents an officer or non-commissioned member from exercising their powers or performing their duties and functions, before the arrival of the civilian authority having jurisdiction in the matter, to the extent necessary to prevent the commission, continuation or repetition of an offence referred to in any of paragraphs 70(d) to (h) that, as the case may be, is being, was, or is alleged to have been committed in Canada, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: making an arrest in relation to the offence in accordance with Division 3 of Part III of this Act or section 494 or 495 of the Criminal Code; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if an arrest is made under paragraph (a), conducting a search incident to the arrest.

Subsection.Text: Nothing in section 70.1 prevents an officer or non-commissioned member from securing or preserving any evidence of or relating to the offence referred to in subsection (1) before the arrival of the civilian authority having jurisdiction in the matter.

Nothing in section 70.1 prevents an officer or non-commissioned member from securing or preserving â to the extent that their powers, duties and functions under this Act, other than under subsection (1) or (2), or any other law authorize them to do so â evidence of or relating to an offence referred to in any of paragraphs 70(d) to (h) that was, or is alleged to have been, committed in Canada.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfer of arrested persons and evidence
Subsection.Text: An officer or non-commissioned member shall, as soon as feasible, transfer a person arrested under paragraph (1)(a) to the custody of the civilian authority having jurisdiction in the matter and transfer to them any evidence secured or preserved under any of subsections (1) to (3).

Section.Text: Nothing in section 70.1 prevents an officer or non-commissioned member from initiating or conducting a private prosecution in relation to an offence referred to in any of paragraphs 70(d) to (h).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 71.16(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Unless they are of the opinion that it is not possible to do so for operational reasons, a commanding officer shall, at the request of the victim or an individual acting on their behalf, appoint an officer or non-commissioned member, who satisfies the conditions established in regulations made by the Governor in Council, to be a liaison officer to assist the victim or individual as provided for in subsection (3). The commanding officer shall, to the extent possible, appoint the officer or non-commissioned member who has been requested by the victim or individual to be their liaison officer.
Paragraphs 71.16(3)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: explaining to the victim or individual acting on their behalf the manner in which service offences are charged, dealt with and tried under the Code of Service Discipline; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: obtaining and transmitting to the victim or individual acting on their behalf information relating to a service offence that they have requested and to which they have a right under this Division.

Section.Text: Subsection 119.1(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Proof of certain facts by certificate
Subsection.Text: In proceedings under subsection (1), a certificate of a person referred to in paragraph 16(2)(b.1) of the Sex Offender Information Registration Act stating that the person named in the certificate failed to report under section 4, 4.1, 4.2 or 4.3 â or provide information under section 5 or notify a person under subsection 6(1) or (1.01) â of that Act is evidence of the statements contained in it without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed it.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 162.5:
A military judge is not to be charged with having committed a service infraction.
Paragraph 162.9(c) of the Act is repealed.
Section 163 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (1) and (2), a military judge may not conduct a hearing.

Section.Text: Section 163.4 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Lâaudience sommaire ne peut commencer que dans les six mois suivant la date de la commission prÃ©sumÃ©e du manquement dâordre militaire auquel elle se rapporte.
Section 165.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may appoint an officer who is a barrister or advocate with at least ten years standing at the bar of a province to be the Director of Military Prosecutions.

The Director of Military Prosecutions holds office during good behaviour for a term of not more than seven years but may be subject to remedial or disciplinary measures in accordance with section 165.101.
The Director of Military Prosecutions is not eligible to be reappointed.

On receipt of a request referred to in subsection 165.101(1), the Governor in Council may, if the Governor in Council is of the opinion that there are exceptional circumstances that justify it, suspend the Director of Military Prosecutions from office until the Governor in Council decides whether to impose any remedial or disciplinary measures.

For the purposes of subsection (4), exceptional circumstances include circumstances in which there are allegations of serious misconduct or allegations related to a risk to occupational health and safety or to a risk of injury to international relations, national defence or national security.

The Minister may request of the Governor in Council that an inquiry be held to determine whether the Director of Military Prosecutions should be subject to remedial or disciplinary measures for any reason set out in paragraphs (12)(a) to (e).
On receipt of a request, the Governor in Council may appoint a judge of a superior court to conduct the inquiry.
The judge has all the powers, rights and privileges that are vested in a superior court, including the power to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: issue a summons requiring any person to appear at the time and place specified in the summons in order to testify about all matters within the personâs knowledge relative to the inquiry and to produce any document or thing relative to the inquiry that the person has or controls; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: administer oaths and examine any person on oath.

Subsection.Text: The judge may engage the services of counsel and other persons having technical or specialized knowledge to assist the judge in conducting the inquiry, establish the terms and conditions of their engagement and, with the approval of the Treasury Board, fix and pay their remuneration and expenses.
Subject to subsections (6) and (7), the inquiry shall be conducted in public.

The judge may, on application, take any measures and make any order that the judge considers necessary to ensure the confidentiality of the inquiry if, after having considered all available alternative measures, the judge is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there is a real and substantial risk that matters involving international relations, national defence or national security will be disclosed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there is a real and substantial risk to the fairness of the inquiry such that the need to prevent disclosure outweighs the societal interest that the inquiry be conducted in public; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: there is a serious possibility that the life, liberty or security of a person will be endangered.

Subsection.Text: If the judge considers it appropriate, the judge may take any measures and make any order that the judge considers necessary to ensure the confidentiality of a hearing held in respect of an application under subsection (6).

The judge is not bound by any legal or technical rules of evidence and may receive, and base a decision on, evidence presented in the proceedings that they consider credible or trustworthy in the circumstances of the case.
An interested party may, with leave of the judge, intervene in the inquiry on any terms and conditions that the judge considers appropriate.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice to Director of Military Prosecutions
Subsection.Text: The Director of Military Prosecutions shall be given reasonable notice of the subject matter of the inquiry and of the time and place of any hearing and shall be given an opportunity, in person or by counsel, to be heard at the hearing, to cross-examine witnesses and to present evidence.
After the inquiry has been completed, the judge shall submit a report containing their findings and recommendations, if any, to the Minister.

The judge may, in the report, recommend that the Director of Military Prosecutions be suspended without pay or removed from office or that any other disciplinary measure or any remedial measure be taken if, in the judgeâs opinion, the Director

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has become incapacitated from the proper execution of that office by reason of infirmity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has committed misconduct;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: has failed in the proper execution of that office;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: has been placed, by conduct or otherwise, in a position that is incompatible with the due execution of that office; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: no longer satisfies the minimum standards and conditions of service applicable to officers.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transmission of report to Governor in Council
Subsection.Text: The Minister shall send the report to the Governor in Council who may, if the Governor in Council considers it appropriate, suspend the Director of Military Prosecutions without pay, remove the Director from office or impose any other disciplinary measure or any remedial measure.

Section.Text: Section 165.16 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the Director of Military Prosecutions is absent or unable to act or the office of Director of Military Prosecutions is vacant, the Minister may authorize any officer who is a barrister or advocate with standing at the bar of a province to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions of the Director of Military Prosecutions, but that officer may act as the Director of Military Prosecutions for a period of more than 90 days only with the approval of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 165.17(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Minister may issue instructions or guidelines in writing in respect of a particular prosecution.
Subsections 165.17(5) and (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Director of Military Prosecutions may, if they consider it to be in the best interests of the administration of military justice, delay making an instruction or guideline issued under subsection (3), or any part of it, available to the public until the completion of the prosecution or any related prosecution.
The Judge Advocate General shall provide the Minister with a copy of every instruction and guideline issued under subsection (2).

Section.Text: Subsection 165.21(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may appoint any officer or non-commissioned member who is a barrister or advocate of at least 10 yearsâ standing at the bar of a province and who has been a member of the Canadian Forces for at least ten years to be a military judge.
The portion of subsection 165.22(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

There is established a Reserve Force Military Judges Panel to which the Governor in Council may name any officer or non-commissioned member who has been a member of the Canadian Forces for at least 10 years and who
Subsection 165.22(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
A member of the Canadian Forces named to the panel is referred to in this Act as a âreserve force military judgeâ.
Subparagraph 183.5(1)(a)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Paragraphs 183.5(2)(a) and (b) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: dâaviser dans les meilleurs dÃ©lais la victime et tout tÃ©moin Ã¢gÃ© de moins de dix-huit ans de leur droit de demander lâordonnance;

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: de rendre lâordonnance, si le procureur de la poursuite, la victime ou tout tÃ©moin Ã¢gÃ© de moins de dix-huit ans lui en fait la demande;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 183.5(2) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (a), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if an order is made, as soon as feasible, inform the victim and witness who are the subject of that order of its existence and of their right to apply to revoke or vary it.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 183.5(4) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (a), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if an order is made, as soon as feasible, inform the victim of the existence of the order and of their right to apply to revoke or vary it.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 183.5(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If the prosecutor makes an application for an order under paragraph (2)(b) or (4)(b), the military judge shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the victim or witness is present, inquire of them if they wish to be the subject of the order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the victim or witness is not present, inquire of the prosecutor if, before the application was made, they determined whether the victim or witness wishes to be the subject of the order; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in any event, advise the prosecutor of their duty under subsection (5.2).

Subsection.Text: If the prosecutor makes the application, they shall, as soon as feasible after the military judge makes the order, inform the military judge that they have

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: informed the victim and the witness who are the subject of the order of its existence;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: determined whether they wish to be the subject of the order; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: informed them of their right to apply to revoke or vary the order.

Subsection.Text: An order made under this section does not apply in either of the following circumstances:

Paragraph.Text: the disclosure of information is made in the course of the administration of military justice and it is made for a purpose other than to make the information known in the community; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure of information is made by a person who is the subject of the order and is about that person and their particulars, in any forum and for any purpose, and they did not intentionally or recklessly reveal the identity of, or reveal particulars likely to identify, any other person whose identity is protected by an order prohibiting the publication in any document or the broadcasting or transmission in any way of information that could identify that other person.

Subsection.Text: An order made under this section does not apply in respect of the disclosure of information by the victim or witness when it is not the purpose of the disclosure to make the information known to the public, including when the disclosure is made to a legal professional, a health care professional or a person in a relationship of trust with the victim or witness.
Subsection 183.6(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An order made under this section does not apply in either of the following circumstances:

Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is made in the course of the administration of military justice and it is made for a purpose other than to make the information known in the community; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure of information is made by a person who is the subject of the order and is about that person and their particulars, in any forum and for any purpose, and they did not intentionally or recklessly reveal the identity of, or reveal particulars likely to identify, any other person whose identity is protected by an order prohibiting the publication in any document or the broadcasting or transmission in any way of information that could identify that other person.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Limitation â victims, witnesses and other participants
Subsection.Text: An order made under this section does not apply in respect of the disclosure of information by the victim, witness or military justice system participant when it is not the purpose of the disclosure to make the information known to the public, including when the disclosure is made to a legal professional, a health care professional or a person in a relationship of trust with the victim, witness or military justice system participant.
Section 183.6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):
If the prosecutor makes an application for an order under subsection (1) or (2), the military judge shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the victim, witness or military justice system participant is present, inquire of them if they wish to be the subject of the order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the victim, witness or military justice system participant is not present, inquire of the prosecutor if, before the application was made, they determined whether the victim, witness or military justice system participant wishes to be the subject of the order; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in any event, advise the prosecutor of their duty under subsection (9.2).

Subsection.Text: Section 183.6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (9):

If an order is made, the military judge shall, as soon as feasible, inform the victim, witness and military justice system participant who are the subject of that order of its existence and of their right to apply to revoke or vary it.
If the prosecutor makes the application, they shall, as soon as feasible after the military judge makes the order, inform the military judge that they have

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: informed the victim, witness and military justice system participant who are the subject of the order of its existence;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: determined whether they wish to be the subject of the order; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: informed them of their right to apply to revoke or vary the order.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 183.6:

Subsection.Text: If a person who is the subject of an order made under section 183.5 or 183.6 requests that the prosecutor have it varied or revoked, the prosecutor shall, as soon as feasible, make an application to vary or revoke the order on their behalf.

If an application to vary or revoke an order made under section 183.5 or 183.6 is made by the person who is the subject of the order or by any other person, including a prosecutor, who is acting on their behalf, the military judge assigned to preside at the court martial or a military judge designated by the Chief Military Judge shall, without holding a hearing, vary or revoke the order, unless the military judge is of the opinion that to do so may affect the privacy interests of any person who is the subject of any order prohibiting the publication in any document or the broadcasting or transmission in any way of information that could identify that person.

If the military judge is of the opinion that varying or revoking the order that is the subject of an application referred to in subsection (2) may affect the privacy interests of any person who is the subject of any order prohibiting the publication in any document or the broadcasting or transmission in any way of information that could identify that person, the military judge shall hold a hearing to determine whether the order should be varied or revoked.

In order to determine whether the order should be varied, the military judge shall take into account whether it is possible to do so in a manner that protects the privacy interests of any other person who is the subject of any order prohibiting the publication in any document or the broadcasting or transmission in any way of information that could identify that person.
The applicant is not required to provide notice of the application to vary or revoke the order to the accused.
The accused shall not be permitted to make submissions in relation to the application.
If the order is varied or revoked, the prosecutor shall notify the accused.
The definition designated offence in section 227 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: designated offenceâmeans a primary offence or a secondary offence.â(infraction dÃ©signÃ©e)

Subsection.Text: Section 227 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) of the definition primary offence in subsection 490.011(1) of the Criminal Code that is punishable under section 130 of this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an attempt or conspiracy to commit an offence referred to in paragraph (a).â(infraction primaire)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence referred to in paragraph (a) of the definition secondary offence in subsection 490.011(1) of the Criminal Code that is punishable under section 130 of this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an attempt or conspiracy to commit an offence referred to in paragraph (a).â(infraction secondaire)

Section.Text: Section 227.01 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (5), when a court martial imposes a sentence on a person in respect of a designated offence, it shall make an order in the prescribed form requiring the person to comply with the Sex Offender Information Registration Act if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a sentence of imprisonment of two years or more is imposed for the designated offence or, if the sentence is in respect of more than one offence, the court martial is of the opinion that a sentence of imprisonment of two years or more is justified for the designated offence; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the victim of the designated offence is under the age of 18 years.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Order â previous offence or obligation
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (5), when a court martial imposes a sentence on a person in respect of a designated offence, it shall make an order in the prescribed form requiring the person to comply with the Sex Offender Information Registration Act if the prosecutor establishes that, before or after the coming into force of paragraphs (a) and (b), the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was previously convicted under section 130 in respect of a primary offence or was previously convicted under the Criminal Code of a primary offence, as defined in subsection 490.011(1) of that Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is or was, as a result of a conviction, subject to an order or obligation under this or another Act of Parliament to comply with the Sex Offender Information Registration Act.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (5), when a court martial imposes a sentence on a person in respect of a designated offence in circumstances in which neither subsection (1) nor (2) applies, or when the court martial finds the person not responsible on account of mental disorder for a designated offence, it shall make an order in the prescribed form requiring the person to comply with the Sex Offender Information Registration Act unless the court martial is satisfied that the person has established that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there would be no connection between making the order and the purpose of helping police services prevent or investigate crimes of a sexual nature by requiring the registration of information relating to sex offenders under that Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the impact of the order on the person, including on their privacy or liberty, would be grossly disproportionate to the public interest in protecting society through the effective prevention or investigation of crimes of a sexual nature that is to be achieved by the registration of information relating to sex offenders under that Act.

Subsection.Text: In determining whether to make an order under subsection (3) in respect of a person, the court martial shall consider

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and seriousness of the designated offence;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the victimâs age and other personal characteristics;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and circumstances of the relationship between the person and the victim;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the personal characteristics and circumstances of the person;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the personâs criminal history, including the age at which they previously committed any offence and the length of time for which they have been at liberty without committing an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the opinions of experts who have examined the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other factors that the court martial considers relevant.

Subsection.Text: A court martial shall make an order under any of subsections (1) to (3) in respect of a secondary offence only if the prosecutor applies for the order and establishes beyond a reasonable doubt that the person committed the secondary offence with the intent to commit a primary offence.
For the purposes of paragraph (2)(a), a previous conviction includes a conviction for an offence

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for which a person is given an adult sentence, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Youth Criminal Justice Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is made in ordinary court, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Young Offenders Act, chapter Y-1 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1985.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 227.02(2) of the Act before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duration of order â subsection 227.01(1) or (3)
Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection 227.01(1) or (3)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsections (3) and (5), ends 10 years after it was made if the maximum term of imprisonment for the offence in connection with which it was made is five years or less;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsections (3) and (5), ends 20 years after it was made if the maximum term of imprisonment for the offence is 10 or 14 years; and

Subsection.Text: Subsections 227.02(2.1) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duration of order â offences in same proceeding
Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection 227.01(1) or (3) applies for life if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the same proceeding, the person has been convicted of, or found not responsible on account of mental disorder for, two or more designated offences in connection with which an order under any of subsections 227.01(1) to (3) may be made; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the court martial is satisfied that those offences demonstrate, or form part of, a pattern of behaviour showing that the person presents an increased risk of reoffending by committing a crime of a sexual nature.

Subsection.Text: If paragraph (3)(a) applies in the circumstances but the court martial is not satisfied as set out in paragraph (3)(b), the duration of the order is determined by applying paragraphs (2)(a) to (c) to the designated offence with the longest maximum term of imprisonment.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duration of order â other order or obligation
Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection 227.01(1) or (3) applies for life if the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was previously convicted of, or previously found not responsible on account of mental disorder for, an offence under section 130 in respect of a primary offence or a primary offence, as defined in subsection 490.011(1) of the Criminal Code, under that Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is, or was at any time, the subject of an order or obligation under this or another Act of Parliament to comply with the Sex Offender Information Registration Act.

Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection 227.01(2) applies for life.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 227.02:
The court martial shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: state the designated offence that forms the basis of an order made under subsection 227.01(1) and the term of imprisonment imposed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: give reasons for a decision under subsection 227.01(3) or paragraph 227.02(3)(b).

Section.Text: If the court martial does not consider the matter under any of subsections 227.01(1) to (3) at the time the sentence is imposed, or a finding of not responsible on account of mental disorder is made, for a primary offence,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Military Judge shall cause the Court Martial Administrator to convene a Standing Court Martial to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Court Martial Administrator shall, within 90 days after the day on which the sentence was imposed or the finding made, convene the court martial;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for greater certainty, the person continues to be liable to be dealt with under the Code of Service Discipline for that purpose; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the court martial may issue a summons in the prescribed form to compel the attendance of the person at the hearing.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 227.03(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if 20 years have elapsed since the order was made, in the case of an order referred to in paragraph 227.02(2)(c) or subsection 227.02(3), (5) or (6).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 227.03(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Pardon, record suspension or absolute discharge
Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (1) and (2), a person may apply for a termination order once they receive a pardon, a record suspension is ordered, or they are absolutely discharged under paragraph 672.54(a) of the Criminal Code.

Section.Text: Subsection 227.04(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The court martial shall make a termination order if it is satisfied that the person has established that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there would be no connection between continuing an order or obligation and the purpose of helping police services prevent or investigate crimes of a sexual nature by requiring the registration of information relating to sex offenders under the Sex Offender Information Registration Act; or

the impact on the person of continuing an order or an obligation, including on their privacy or liberty, would be grossly disproportionate to the public interest in protecting society through the effective prevention or investigation of crimes of a sexual nature that is to be achieved by the registration of information relating to sex offenders under that Act.

Subsection.Text: In determining whether to make the termination order, the court martial shall consider

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and seriousness of the offence that is the basis of an order or obligation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the victimâs age and other personal characteristics;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and circumstances of the relationship between the person and the victim;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the personal characteristics and circumstances of the person;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the personâs criminal history, including the age at which they previously committed any offence and the length of time for which they have been at liberty without committing an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the opinions of experts who have examined the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other factors that the court martial considers relevant.

Section.Text: Section 227.07 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The reference in subsection (1) to the definition designated offence is a reference to that definition as it read from time to time before the day on which this subsection comes into force, the reference in paragraph (2)(c) to subsection 227.01(3) is a reference to that provision as it read from time to time before that day and the reference in that paragraph to subsection 490.012(3) of the Criminal Code is a reference to that provision as it read from time to time before October 26, 2023.

Section.Text: Section 227.09 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: In paragraph (3)(d), the reference to the definition designated offence in section 227 of this Act is a reference to that definition as it read from time to time before the day on which this subsection comes into force and the reference to the definition designated offence in subsection 490.011(1) of the Criminal Code is a reference to that definition as it read from time to time before October 26, 2023.
The portion of subsection 227.12(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Time for application â single offence
Subsection.Text: A person may apply for a termination order if the following period has elapsed since they were sentenced, or found not responsible on account of mental disorder, for the offence listed in the notice:
Subsections 227.12(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If more than one offence is listed in the notice, the person may apply for a termination order if 20 years have elapsed since they were sentenced, or found not responsible on account of mental disorder, for the most recent offence.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Pardon or record suspension or absolute discharge
Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (2) and (3), a person may apply for a termination order once they receive a pardon, a record suspension is ordered, or they are absolutely discharged under paragraph 672.54(a) of the Criminal Code.

Section.Text: Subsection 227.13(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The court martial shall make an order terminating the obligation if it is satisfied that the person has established that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there would be no connection between continuing the obligation and the purpose of helping police services prevent or investigate crimes of a sexual nature by requiring the registration of information relating to sex offenders under the Sex Offender Information Registration Act; or

the impact on the person of continuing the obligation, including on their privacy or liberty, would be grossly disproportionate to the public interest in protecting society through the effective prevention or investigation of crimes of a sexual nature that is to be achieved by the registration of information relating to sex offenders under the that Act.

Subsection.Text: In determining whether to make the termination order, the court martial shall consider

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and seriousness of the offence that is the basis of the obligation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the victimâs age and other personal characteristics;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and circumstances of the relationship between the person and the victim;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the personal characteristics and circumstances of the person;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the personâs criminal history, including the age at which they previously committed any offence and the length of time for which they have been at liberty without committing an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the opinions of experts who have examined the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other factors that the court martial considers relevant.

Section.Text: Paragraph 227.15(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: appeal the legality of a decision made under section 227.01 or 227.02, subsection 227.04(1), 227.1(4) or 227.13(1) or section 227.22 or 227.23 of this Act â or appeal a decision made under section 490.012 or 490.013 or subsection 490.016(1), 490.023(2), 490.027(1), 490.02905(2) or (2.2), 490.029051(2) or (3), 490.02909(1), 490.029111(2), 490.029112(2) or (3), 490.02913(1), 490.04(4) or (5) or 490.05(4) or (5) of the Criminal Code â within the required period;

Section.Text: Paragraph 227.18(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of a proceeding under section 227.01 or 227.02, subsection 227.04(1), 227.1(4) or 227.13(1) or section 227.22 or 227.23 or for the purpose of an appeal respecting the legality of a decision made under any of those provisions, whether a person who is subject to the Code of Service Discipline, or who is an officer, or non-commissioned member, of the primary reserve, is, or was at any time, required to comply with an order made under section 227.01 of this Act or section 490.012 of the Criminal Code or with an obligation under section 227.06 of this Act, section 490.019 or 490.02901 of the Criminal Code or section 36.1 of the International Transfer of Offenders Act;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 227.21:

Subsection.Text: A person may apply for an order exempting them from an order made under section 227.01 on or after April 15, 2011 but before the day on which this subsection comes into force.

The application shall be made to the Chief Military Judge if the applicant is subject to the Code of Service Discipline or is an officer, or non-commissioned member, of the primary reserve at the time. In any other case, the application shall be made to a court under section 490.04 of the Criminal Code.
On receipt of the application, the Chief Military Judge shall cause the Court Martial Administrator to convene a Standing Court Martial to try the issue.
The court martial shall not make an exemption order in respect of an application made under subsection (1) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the sentence for the designated offence that is the basis of the order made under section 227.01 is a term of imprisonment of two years or more â or, if the sentence is in respect of multiple offences, one of which is the designated offence, the court martial was, at the time of imposing the sentence, of the opinion that a sentence of imprisonment of two years or more was justified for the designated offence â and the victim of the designated offence was under the age of 18 years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: before or after the order under section 227.01 was made, the person

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was convicted of an offence under section 130 in respect of a primary offence â or was convicted under the Criminal Code of a primary offence, as defined in subsection 490.011(1) of that Act â that is not the offence on the basis of which the order was made, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is or was, as a result of a conviction, subject to another order under this or another Act of Parliament to comply with the Sex Offender Information Registration Act.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (4), the court martial shall make an exemption order if it is satisfied that the person has established that, at the time the order was made,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there was no connection between the order and the purpose of helping police services prevent or investigate crimes of a sexual nature by requiring the registration of information relating to sex offenders under the Sex Offender Information Registration Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the impact of the order on the person, including on their privacy or liberty, was grossly disproportionate to the public interest in protecting society through the effective prevention or investigation of crimes of a sexual nature that is to be achieved by the registration of information relating to sex offenders under that Act.

Subsection.Text: In determining whether to make the exemption order, the court martial shall consider

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and seriousness of the offence that is the basis of the order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the victimâs age and other personal characteristics;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and circumstances of the relationship between the person and the victim;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the personal characteristics and circumstances of the person;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the personâs criminal history, including the age at which they previously committed any offence and the length of time for which they have been at liberty without committing an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the opinions of experts who have examined the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other factors that the court martial considers relevant.

Subsection.Text: The court martial shall give reasons for its decision.

If the court martial makes an exemption order, it shall also make an order requiring the Royal Canadian Mounted Police to permanently remove from the database all information that relates to the person that was registered in the database on receipt of a copy of the order made under section 227.01.
A person may apply for an order to vary the duration of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under section 227.01, if the order applies for life under subsection 227.02(2.1), as it read from time to time before the day on which this paragraph comes into force; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an obligation under section 227.06, if the obligation applies for life under paragraph 227.09(3)(d) and none of the offences listed in the prescribed form served on the person in respect of the obligation have a maximum term of imprisonment for life.

Subsection.Text: The application shall be made to the Chief Military Judge if the applicant is subject to the Code of Service Discipline or is an officer, or non-commissioned member, of the primary reserve at the time. In any other case, the application shall be made to a court under section 490.05 of the Criminal Code.
On receipt of the application, the Chief Military Judge shall cause the Court Martial Administrator to convene a Standing Court Martial to try the issue.

The court martial shall make the variation order if it is satisfied that the person has established that the offences that are the basis of the order or obligation do not demonstrate, or do not form part of, a pattern of behaviour showing that the person presents an increased risk of reoffending by committing a crime of a sexual nature.
If the court martial makes a variation order, it shall set out the duration of the order or obligation in the order, which shall be determined by applying

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 227.02(2) to the offence in question with the longest maximum term of imprisonment, in the case of an order; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 227.09(3)(a) and (b) to the offence in question with the longest maximum term of imprisonment, in the case of an obligation.

Subsection.Text: The court martial shall give reasons for its decision.
The court martial shall cause the Provost Marshal to be notified of a variation order made under this section.

Section.Text: Paragraph 230(g) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the legality of a decision made under section 227.01 or 227.02;

Section.Text: Paragraph 230.1(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the legality of a decision made under section 227.01 or 227.02;

Section.Text: Section 230.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection 232(3), a person who applied for an exemption order under section 227.1 or 227.22, a termination order under section 227.03 or 227.12 or a variation order under section 227.23 and the Minister or counsel instructed by the Minister have the right to appeal to the Court Martial Appeal Court in respect of the legality of the decision made by the court martial.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 240.5(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

On the hearing of an appeal respecting the legality of a decision made under section 227.01 or 227.02 or subsection 227.04(1), 227.1(4) or 227.13(1) the Court Martial Appeal Court, or another court hearing the appeal, may

Paragraph.Text: dismiss the appeal; or
allow the appeal and order a new hearing, quash or amend the order or make an order that may be made under the applicable provision.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Appeal against decision â section 227.22 or 227.23
Subsection.Text: On the hearing of an appeal respecting the legality of a decision made under section 227.22 or 227.23, the Court Martial Appeal Court, or another court hearing the appeal, may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: dismiss the appeal; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: allow the appeal and order a new hearing, quash or amend the exemption order or variation order or make an order that may be made, as the case may be, under subsection 227.22(5) or section 227.23.

Subsection.Text: If the Court Martial Appeal Court or other court makes an order that may be made under section 227.01, it shall cause the requirements set out in section 227.05 to be fulfilled.
Subsections 240.5(3) and (4) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Si elle rend une ordonnance en application des paragraphes 227.04(1) ou 227.13(1), la Cour dâappel de la cour martiale â ou toute autre juridiction saisie de lâappel â veille Ã ce que le grand prÃ©vÃ´t en soit avisÃ©.

Si elle rend une ordonnance de dispense en application du paragraphe 227.1(4), la Cour dâappel de la cour martiale â ou toute autre juridiction saisie de lâappel â rend aussi lâordonnance prÃ©vue au paragraphe 227.1(6).
Section 240.5 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
If the Court Martial Appeal Court or other court makes an exemption order that may be made under section 227.22, it shall also make the order referred to in subsection 227.22(8).

If the Court Martial Appeal Court or other court quashes an exemption order made under section 227.22, it shall cause the Provost Marshal to be notified of the decision and shall cause the person who applied for the order to be informed of section 119.1 and sections 4 to 7.1 of the Sex Offender Information Registration Act.

If the Court Martial Appeal Court or other court quashes or amends a variation order made under section 227.23, or makes an order that may be made under that section, it shall cause the Provost Marshal to be notified of the decision.

Section.Text: Section 249.18 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may appoint an officer who is a barrister or advocate with at least ten years standing at the bar of a province to be the Director of Defence Counsel Services.

The Director of Defence Counsel Services holds office during good behaviour for a term of not more than seven years but may be subject to remedial or disciplinary measures in accordance with section 249.181.
The Director of Defence Counsel Services is not eligible to be reappointed.

On receipt of a request referred to in subsection 249.181(1), the Governor in Council may, if the Governor in Council is of the opinion that there are exceptional circumstances that justify it, suspend the Director of Defence Counsel Services from office until the Governor in Council decides whether to impose any remedial or disciplinary measures.

For the purposes of subsection (4), exceptional circumstances include circumstances in which there are allegations of serious misconduct or allegations related to a risk to occupational health and safety or to a risk of injury to international relations, national defence or national security.

The Minister may request of the Governor in Council that an inquiry be held to determine whether the Director of Defence Counsel Services should be subject to remedial or disciplinary measures for any reason set out in paragraphs (12)(a) to (e).
On receipt of a request, the Governor in Council may appoint a judge of a superior court to conduct the inquiry.
The judge has all the powers, rights and privileges that are vested in a superior court, including the power to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: issue a summons requiring any person to appear at the time and place specified in the summons in order to testify about all matters within the personâs knowledge relative to the inquiry and to produce any document or thing relative to the inquiry that the person has or controls; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: administer oaths and examine any person on oath.

Subsection.Text: The judge may engage the services of counsel and other persons having technical or specialized knowledge to assist the judge in conducting the inquiry, establish the terms and conditions of their engagement and, with the approval of the Treasury Board, fix and pay their remuneration and expenses.
Subject to subsections (6) and (7), the inquiry shall be conducted in public.

The judge may, on application, take any measures and make any order that the judge considers necessary to ensure the confidentiality of the inquiry if, after having considered all available alternative measures, the judge is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there is a real and substantial risk that matters involving international relations, national defence or national security will be disclosed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there is a real and substantial risk to the fairness of the inquiry such that the need to prevent disclosure outweighs the societal interest that the inquiry be conducted in public; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: there is a serious possibility that the life, liberty or security of a person will be endangered.

Subsection.Text: If the judge considers it appropriate, the judge may take any measures and make any order that the judge considers necessary to ensure the confidentiality of a hearing held in respect of an application under subsection (6).

The judge is not bound by any legal or technical rules of evidence and may receive, and base a decision on, evidence presented in the proceedings that they consider credible or trustworthy in the circumstances of the case.
An interested party may, with leave of the judge, intervene in the inquiry on any terms and conditions that the judge considers appropriate.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice to Director of Defence Counsel Services
Subsection.Text: The Director of Defence Counsel Services shall be given reasonable notice of the subject matter of the inquiry and of the time and place of any hearing and shall be given an opportunity, in person or by counsel, to be heard at the hearing, to cross-examine witnesses and to present evidence.
After the inquiry has been completed, the judge shall submit a report containing their findings and recommendations, if any, to the Minister.

The judge may, in the report, recommend that the Director of Defence Counsel Services be suspended without pay or removed from office or that any other disciplinary measure or any remedial measure be taken if, in the judgeâs opinion, the Director

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has become incapacitated from the proper execution of that office by reason of infirmity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has committed misconduct;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: has failed in the proper execution of that office;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: has been placed, by conduct or otherwise, in a position that is incompatible with the due execution of that office; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: no longer satisfies the minimum standards and conditions of service applicable to officers.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transmission of report to Governor in Council
Subsection.Text: The Minister shall send the report to the Governor in Council who may, if the Governor in Council considers it appropriate, suspend the Director of Defence Counsel Services without pay, remove the Director from office or impose any other disciplinary measure or any remedial measure.

Section.MarginalNote: Acting Director of Defence Counsel Services
Section.Text: If the Director of Defence Counsel Services is absent or unable to act or the office of Director of Defence Counsel Services is vacant, the Minister may authorize any officer who is a barrister or advocate with standing at the bar of a province to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions of the Director of Defence Counsel Services, but that officer may act as the Director of Defence Counsel Services for a period of more than 90 days only with the approval of the Governor in Council.
The definition interference complaint in section 250 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: interference complaintâmeans a complaint made under subsection 250.19(1) or (1.1).â(plainte pour ingÃ©rence)

Section.Text: Subsection 250.19(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any member of the military police or person performing policing duties or functions under the Provost Marshal Generalâs supervision who believes on reasonable grounds that any officer, non-commissioned member or senior official of the Department has improperly interfered with the performance of any of the policing duties or functions that are prescribed for the purposes of section 250.18 in regulations made by the Governor in Council shall make a complaint about that person under this Division.

If they believe on reasonable grounds that an officer, non-commissioned member or senior official of the Department has improperly interfered with the performance of any of the policing duties or functions that are prescribed for the purposes of section 250.18 in regulations made by the Governor in Council, any of the following may make a complaint about that person under this Division:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a victim or other person affected by the performance of the policing duty or function; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an individual acting on behalf of a victim referred to in paragraph (a).

Section.Text: Subsection 250.26(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a conduct complaint is about the conduct of the Provost Marshal General, the Minister, or a person designated by the Minister, is responsible for dealing with the complaint and has all the powers and duties of the Provost Marshal General under this Division.

Section.Text: Subsection 250.49(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Provost Marshal General is the subject of the complaint, the review shall be conducted by the Minister.

Section.Text: Subsection 250.5(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Chief of the Defence Staff, the Deputy Minister or the Provost Marshal General is the subject of the complaint, the review shall be conducted by the Minister.

Section.Text: Section 303.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: A prosecutor shall not commence or continue a prosecution against a person who is the subject of the order unless, in the opinion of the prosecutor,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person knowingly failed to comply with the order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the privacy interests of another person who is the subject of any order prohibiting the publication in any document or the broadcasting or transmission in any way of information that could identify that person have been compromised; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a warning to the individual is not appropriate.

Paragraph.Label: (z.2)
